name: bleno

documentation.readme: A Node.js module for implementing BLE (Bluetooth Low Energy) peripherals.
usage_sections: disabled, if BlueZ 5.14 or later is installed. Use to power Bluetooth adapter up after stopping or disabling .
install Node.js using the NodeSource package

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Print the tool version and exit.

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

arguments.name: --capture
arguments.description: Capture mode: write packets to a pcap file.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [ble_4_x, ble_5_x, ble_mesh] in the Bluetooth category. Fits when the engagement needs: [![Gitter](https://badges.gitter.im/Join%20Chat.svg)](https://gitter.im/sandeepmistry/bleno?utm_source=badge&utm_medium=badge&utm_campaign=pr-badge&utm_content=badge).
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: A Node.js module for implementing BLE (Bluetooth Low Energy) peripherals.
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: npm install bleno. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `Disable automatic loading of the default Bluetooth stack by putting [no-ubt.conf]($KFIOSA_TARGET_URL into`. Further invocations from upstream: `Set the primary services available on the peripheral.`; `sudo BLENO_HCI_DEVICE_ID=1 node <your file>.js`.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.
